title: Curve-Mesh Conversion Nodes
description: Implement 5 nodes that convert between curves and meshes. CurveToMesh: sweep a profile curve along a path curve to generate a mesh (essential for organic modeling -- tubes, limbs, fingers). CurveToPoints: sample points along a curve. FillCurve: create a mesh face from a closed planar curve. MeshToCurve: extract boundary edges of a mesh as curves. PointsToCurves: connect ordered points into curve splines. CurveToMesh is the most important node in this ticket -- it enables creating tubular geometry from curves, which is fundamental for organic procedural modeling.
definition-of-done: 1. Five node classes in nodes/conversion/. 2. CurveToMesh generates a clean manifold mesh by sweeping a profile along a path. 3. CurveToPoints outputs vertex positions and tangents along the curve. 4. FillCurve triangulates a closed planar curve into a mesh face. 5. MeshToCurve extracts boundary edges as curve splines. 6. PointsToCurves creates Splines from ordered point lists.
testing-plan: 1. CurveToMesh: sweep a circle profile along a line path, verify a cylinder-like mesh. 2. CurveToMesh: sweep a circle along a curved path, verify a bent tube with no self-intersection. 3. FillCurve on a square: verify 2 triangles (or appropriate triangulation). 4. MeshToCurve on an open plane: verify boundary edges extracted as a curve. 5. CurveToPoints on a circle with count=8: verify 8 evenly spaced points.
todos: Implement CurveToMeshNode: compute Frenet frames along path, sweep profile at each frame, connect into mesh
Handle CurveToMesh twist prevention (minimize rotation between frames)
Implement CurveToPointsNode with count or spacing parameter
Implement FillCurveNode using ear-clipping or Delaunay triangulation for planar curves
Implement MeshToCurveNode: find boundary half-edges, order them into splines
unknowns: Whether CurveToMesh should support scaling the profile along the path (taper) or if that requires a separate attribute
How to handle non-planar curves in FillCurve (project to best-fit plane?)
